info.title: Workfront Fusion API
info.description:
REST APIs for managing and monitoring your Workfront Fusion resources. Use these APIs to programmatically access activity logs and other Fusion components coming soon.

**Base URL:** https://fusion.adobe.io

**Getting Started:**

 For detailed setup instructions, make sure to visit the [Getting Started guide](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/).
1. Set up authentication in Adobe Developer Console, as documented [here](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/authentication/).
2. Obtain required headers (Authorization, x-api-key, x-organization-id, x-gw-region). More details [here](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/api_headers/).
3. Update permissions for the technical account, as documented [here](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/technical_accounts/).
4. Make your first API call.

tags.name: Scenarios
tags.description: Endpoints for listing, retrieving, cloning, and inspecting scenario dependencies in your organization, and related scenario operations.

tags.name: Folders
tags.description: Endpoints for creating and managing folders used to organize automations within teams.

tags.name: Activity Logs
tags.description: Endpoints for getting and exporting log of activities, such as creating a scenario or inviting a user, for your organization. Please note, for accessing activity logs, user must be a Workfront Fusion administrator for the organization.

externalDocs.description: Find out more in product documentation.

tags.name: Operations
tags.description: Endpoints for getting operation count in a particular Fusion organization, grouped by various parameters. An operation is a task performed by a module. For tracking purposes, any successful action performed by a module is an operation.

externalDocs.description: Find out more in product documentation.

tags.name: Connections
tags.description: Endpoints for managing connections within your organization. These endpoints allow you to list all connections, retrieve details for a specific connection, and view scenarios associated with a given connection.

tags.name: Hooks
tags.description: Endpoints for managing hooks within your organization. These endpoints allow you to list all hooks scoped to a team and retrieve full details and configuration for a specific hook.

tags.name: Data Stores
tags.description: Endpoints for managing data stores within your organization. These endpoints allow you to list all data stores scoped to a team, and retrieve details for a specific data store including its metadata and usage metrics such as record count and storage size.

tags.name: Logs
tags.description: Endpoints for retrieving execution logs within your organization. These endpoints allow you to list all executions scoped to a scenario or incomplete execution.

post.description:
Creates a copy of the scenario in the target team, applying dependency replacements from the body.
The response body contains the new scenario ID; the `Location` header points to the new resource.

**Rules**
- **Same team**: existing connections may be reused; you can omit replacements for them.
- **Different team**: connections cannot be reused; provide replacements for each required connection.
- If the source scenario uses a **hook**, provide an unused hook in the replacements.
- For **data stores, data structures, and keys**: if you supply IDs in the request, those are used; otherwise they are duplicated in the target team.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description: Source scenario ID to clone

teamId.description: Team ID where the cloned scenario will be created

folderId.description: Folder ID where the cloned scenario will be created

replacements.description: Maps source dependencies to target resources in the new team

sourceId.description: Source dependency id (in the original scenario)

targetId.description: Target dependency id (replacement in the clone)

201.description: New scenario created; response body contains its ID. `Location` header is set to the new scenario URL.

id.description: ID of the newly created (cloned) scenario

get.description:
Exports activity logs as CSV or XLSX based on the Accept header. Use this endpoint to download activity logs for offline analysis, compliance reporting, or integration with external tools.

**Export Formats:**

- **CSV**: Set `Accept: text/csv` (default)
- **Excel**: Set `Accept: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`

**Note:** Large exports may take several seconds to generate.

get.description:
Retrieves operation counts for an organization, optionally filtered by team, scenario, and/or package, with optional grouping by dimension.

**Grouping:** Use the `groupby` parameter to break down operation counts by `team`, `scenario`, `package`, or `module`. When set, each time-period entry in the response includes a nested array broken down by the chosen group.

**Date Range:** The `from` and `to` parameters define the time window. The maximum allowed range is 1 year. Results are automatically grouped by `day` or `month` depending on the range.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Start date in ISO 8601 format (UTC). Defines the beginning of the date range for operation counts.

**Note:** Date range between `from` and `to` cannot exceed 1 year.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
End date in ISO 8601 format (UTC). Defines the end of the date range for operation counts.

**Note:** Date range between `from` and `to` cannot exceed 1 year.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: **Deprecated** — use the `x-organization-id` request header instead. Accepted for backward compatibility; will be removed in a future release.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: Filter results to a specific team. When provided, only operations for this team are returned.

parameters.name: scenarioId
parameters.description:
Filter results to a specific scenario.

**Note:** Requires `teamId` to also be specified.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description:
Filter results to a specific package name.

**Note:** Requires `scenarioId` to also be specified.

parameters.name: groupby
parameters.description:
Group operation counts by a specific dimension. When set, each time-period entry in the response includes a nested array broken down by the chosen group.

**Available Groups:**
- `module` - Group by module name
- `package` - Group by package name
- `scenario` - Group by scenario ID
- `team` - Group by team ID

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description:
Sort results by timestamp. Prefix with `+` for ascending or `-` for descending.

**Values:**
- `+timestamp` - Oldest first (ascending)
- `-timestamp` - Newest first (descending)

parameters.name: property
parameters.description:
```
Property filters. Can be repeated and are AND-ed together.

Allowed fields:
- id
- status
- timestamp
- duration
- bundles
- operations
- transfer

Supported operators:
- ==  (equals)
- !=  (not equals)
- >   (greater than)
- >=  (greater than or equal)
- <   (less than)
- <=  (less than or equal)
- ~   (case-insensitive regex match)

Special cases:
- field           → field exists
- !field          → field does not exist
```

example: teamId==3849

status.description: Execution result status code: `0` running, `1` success, `2` warning, `3` error.

timestamp.description: Execution start time, as an ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC.

duration.description: Total execution wall-clock time, in milliseconds.

verifier.description: True for manual runs, which are run to verify/test the automation; false for automatic runs.

bundles.description: Total number of data bundles processed across all modules during the execution.

operations.description: Total number of module operations performed during the execution.

transfer.description: Total amount of data transferred during the execution, in bytes.

modules.description: Per-module operation statistics. Each entry contains `package` (app/connector package), `module` (module name), and `operations` (operations performed by that module).

processing.description: True while the execution is still being processed; false once it has completed. Logs may be partial while this is true.

config.description: Scenario runtime configuration snapshot. Boolean flags: `dlq`, `instant`, `dataloss`, `listener`, `parallel`, `autoCommit`, `sequential`, `localAccess`, `confidential`. Numbers: `roundtrips`, `maxFileSize` (bytes), `timeout` (milliseconds).

start.description: Cursor value of the first item in the current page

next.description: Cursor value for retrieving the next page

property.description: Applied `property` filter expression(s) from the request
example: teamId==304

type.description: Resource type filter

count.description: Number of items in the current page

next.description: URL to the next page of results

id.description: Unique identifier for the execution. A 32-character lowercase string.

type.description: How the execution was run. `auto` = scheduled/automatic run, `manual` = user-initiated run-once.

kind.description: Type of entity this execution belongs to. `scenario` = automation scenario, `dlq` = incomplete execution.

kindId.description: Identifier of the parent entity referenced by `kind` (the scenario or DLQ id). Numeric id represented as a string.

teamId.description: Identifier of the team that owns the parent automation.

organizationId.description: Identifier of the organization that owns the execution.

authorId.description: Identifier of the user who authored/initiated the execution. Absent for fully automated runs with no acting user.

duration.description: Total execution wall-clock time, in milliseconds.

verifier.description: True for manual runs, which are run to verify/test the automation; false for automatic runs.

operations.description: Total number of module operations performed during the execution.

Authorization.description:
**Required.** IMS access token for authenticating the user or technical account making the request.

See the [Authentication guide](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/authentication/) for detailed instructions on obtaining access tokens.

[API Headers Documentation](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/api_headers/)

Organization ID.name: x-organization-id
Organization ID.description:
**Required.** Your Fusion Organization ID. Must be a number. Scopes all requests to your specific Fusion organization. You can find this ID in the Fusion Org Overview page URL: `/organization/{organizationId}/dashboard`.

**Example:** `12345`

[API Headers Documentation](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/api_headers/)

API Key.name: x-api-key
API Key.description:
**Required.** Your application's Client ID from Adobe Developer Console. This identifies your application and is the same value used for IMS authentication.

[API Headers Documentation](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/api_headers/)

Region.name: x-gw-region
Region.description:
**Required.** Routes requests to the correct Fusion regional instance.

**Example:** `or2`

[API Headers Documentation](https://developer-stage.adobe.com/workfront-fusion-apis/getting-started/api_headers/)
